title: 10 best herbal essential oils for inflamed gums
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for inflamed gums are natural extracts derived from plants that help alleviate gum inflammation, discomfort, and pain. These oils contain antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties that combat gum infections and promote oral health. The best herbal essential oils for inflamed gums include Melaleuca alternifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Syzygium aromaticum, Salvia officinalis, Commiphora molmol, Cinnamomum zeylanicum, Cymbopogon citratus, and Curcuma longa. If left untreated, inflamed gums can lead to severe tooth loss, difficulty eating, and low self-esteem, negatively impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its potent antibacterial properties, which can help eliminate the bacteria that cause gum inflammation. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce swelling and ease pain, promoting a healthy and comfortable oral environment. Additionally, tea tree oil's antiseptic properties help prevent further infection, allowing the gums to heal and recover naturally. As a result, incorporating tea tree essential oils into your oral care routine can provide effective relief from inflamed gums.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tea Tree Oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which can contribute to gum inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The oil's active compounds, such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.
Antioxidant properties: Tea Tree Oil contains antioxidants that can help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antimicrobial compounds that help combat bacteria and reduce inflammation, making them an effective treatment for gum infections.
Barks: Containing terpenes with anti-inflammatory properties, barks can help soothe and calm irritated gum tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine 500 grams of the washed leaves with 1000 milliliters of distilled water in a large glass container.
Steam distill the mixture for 90 minutes, collecting the essential oil as it separates from the aqueous phase.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and contaminants.
Store the filtered melaleuca alternifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, derived from its rich concentration of eucalyptol and cineole. These compounds work to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums, allowing for a more comfortable and manageable experience. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and viral infections that can contribute to inflamed gums, promoting healthy oral care and a reduction in inflammation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.
Antimicrobial: The oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that may be contributing to gum inflammation and infections.

Wound healing: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has been shown to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and reducing scar tissue formation, which can aid in the recovery of inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, Tasmanian blue gum roots have been traditionally used to soothe and calm inflamed gums.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian blue gum contain powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents that help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning for optimal oil content and minimal contamination.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves thoroughly using a clean towel or air blower within 24 hours of harvesting.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a traditional steam distillation apparatus with a water-to-herb ratio of 1:4 within 6-8 hours.
Collect the essential oil fractions within 2-3 minutes after distillation and store in dark glass containers at room temperature for up to 12 months.
Filter the collected essential oil using a paper or cloth filter to remove any impurities or sediment before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce swelling and redness in the gums, promoting a healthy environment for gum tissue to heal. Additionally, lemongrass essential oils have been shown to stimulate blood circulation, which helps to remove toxins and promote oxygenation of the gums, further aiding in the reduction of inflammation and gum disease.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemongrass essential oil has antibacterial properties that help combat bacteria that can cause gum inflammation and infection, promoting a healthy mouth environment.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass essential oil reduce swelling and redness associated with inflamed gums, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall oral health and preventing further inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemongrass roots are valued for their ability to reduce gum inflammation and prevent further damage.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stem (rhizome) of lemongrass contains high levels of citral, a natural antiseptic that helps combat bacterial infections causing gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves from a pesticide-free garden or purchase dried form in bulk quantities.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 6 hours to preserve their therapeutic properties.
Steam distill 250g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves with 1 liter of distilled water for 4 hours to extract essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation mixture using a separation funnel and collect in dark glass bottles.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or a paper towel to remove any impurities before storing at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation. When applied topically or consumed orally, these compounds can penetrate deep into the gum tissue, targeting the source of the inflammation and providing rapid relief from discomfort. As a result, herbal ginger essential oils offer a safe and effective natural remedy for soothing inflamed gums.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Ginger oil helps reduce pain and inflammation in the gums by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and swelling.
Antioxidant Activity: Ginger oil's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to inflammation, promoting a healthy balance in the oral cavity.

Wound Healing Properties: Ginger oil's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds accelerate wound healing by enhancing collagen synthesis, improving tissue repair, and reducing bacterial growth in the gums.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems, are rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate gum pain.
Roots: Ginger roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol, which can effectively combat inflamed gums and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger rhizomes of zingiber officinale roots weighing approximately 2 kilograms.
Dry the harvested ginger rhizomes in a warm and dry environment at room temperature for 3-4 days.
Steam distill the dried ginger rhizomes using 300 grams of distilled water and 1 kilogram of the dried ginger root material.
Separate the essential oil from the resulting distillation mixture by decanting it into a glass container and discarding the solids.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The menthol content in peppermint oil also has antibacterial properties, making it effective in combating the underlying causes of gum inflammation. When applied topically or used as a mouthwash, peppermint essential oils can provide quick relief from inflamed gums, promoting healthy gums and fresh breath. Its calming effects can also help to soothe sensitive teeth and gums, providing a refreshing oral care experience.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Peppermint essential oil has antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that contribute to gum inflammation, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healthy gum tissue.

Anti-inflammatory property: Peppermint essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with inflamed gums, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a smoother healing process.

Antioxidant property: Peppermint essential oil contains antioxidants that can help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum inflammation and disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help soothe and calm irritated gum tissue.
Leaves: Fresh peppermint leaves contain menthol and other active compounds that help reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the early morning, ensuring a minimum of 20% menthol content.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by gently rinsing with cool water and blotting dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture.
Steam distill the cleaned plant material using a clevenger apparatus at 100°c for 30-40 minutes, yielding approximately 0.5-1.5% essential oil content.
Filter the distillate through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass containers, discarding any solids and storing the liquid in a cool, dry place.
Allow the essential oil to mature for at least 24 hours before use, shaking the container occasionally to facilitate decantation of impurities and sediment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like camphor, bornyl acetate, and thujone that have been shown to reduce inflammation and eliminate the bacteria that cause gum disease. When applied topically or inhaled, sage essential oil can help reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with inflamed gums, promoting a healthy mouth and overall well-being. Its analgesic properties also make it an effective natural remedy for relieving toothache pain.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Sage essential oil has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause gum inflammation and infections, reducing the risk of further complications.
Anti-inflammatory: Sage essential oil contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce swelling, redness, and pain in inflamed gums, promoting healing and comfort.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of sage essential oil help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gum inflammation and damage, protecting the gums from further harm and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to reduce inflammation and relieve gum pain, making them a valuable component for medicinal essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves from a pesticide-free garden or purchase high-quality dried leaves.
Combine 100g of harvested leaves with 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 48 hours in a dark place at room temperature before straining the liquid.
Transfer the infused liquid to a steam distillation apparatus and heat it to 100°c for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the resulting essential oil by condensing the vaporized molecules and transfer it to a glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its potent antibacterial properties that target the root causes of gum inflammation. The oil's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gingivitis and periodontitis. When applied topically, thyme essential oils can help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with inflamed gums, promoting a healthy gum environment and reducing the risk of further complications.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme essential oil's antimicrobial properties help combat the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that contribute to gum inflammation by reducing the biofilm that forms on teeth and gums.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme essential oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums, promoting a healthy environment for oral tissues.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and free radicals that can damage gum tissue, leading to inflammation, and also enhance the body's natural defense mechanisms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.
Flowers: Thyme flowers possess antibacterial properties that combat bacteria responsible for gum infections, promoting healthy gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from your garden or local market in the early morning.
Dry the harvested leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for 7-10 days.
Place 100g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a glass jar and cover with 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily before straining and filtering the liquid.
Transfer the resulting essential oil to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's compounds, such as camphor and bornyl acetate, work to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with inflamed gums. Additionally, rosemary's antimicrobial properties help combat the underlying bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation. By applying a few drops of rosemary essential oil to the affected area or massaging it into the gum tissue, individuals can experience relief from inflamed gums and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Rosemary essential oil's antibacterial compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help combat the growth of bacteria that can cause gum inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, like rosmarinic acid, reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums, providing relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: Rosemary essential oil's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to inflammation, protecting the health of the mouth and gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the rosemary plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves are rich in camphor and bornyl acetate, two compounds that have analgesic and antiseptic properties, making them effective against gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning for optimal oil content retention.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves to prevent contamination and spoilage during processing.
Steam distill 20 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves at 100°c for 60 minutes to extract essential oils.
Filter the distillate through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment within 30 minutes.
Transfer the filtered essential oil to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Basil essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's compounds, such as eugenol and linalool, have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria that can cause gum inflammation and disease. Additionally, basil oil's analgesic and soothing properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with inflamed gums, promoting a healthy and comfortable oral environment.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Basil essential oil helps combat oxidative stress and inflammation in gum tissue, reducing the risk of gum disease and promoting healthy oral health.

Rhyzomes: Basil's rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate gum swelling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ocimum basilicum leaves and flowers in large quantities.
Steam distill the plant material using water and steam for 30-40 minutes.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the condensed steam into a collection vessel.
Filter the oil through activated charcoal or diatomaceous earth to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the extracted ocimum basilicum essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allspice essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat harmful bacteria that can cause gum disease. When applied topically or added to mouthwash, allspice essential oils help soothe and calm irritated gums, promoting healthy gums and a fresh breath. remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Allspice essential oil contains compounds like eugenol and caryophyllene, which have antimicrobial effects that can help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that contribute to gum inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The oil's phenolic content, including caryophyllene and linalool, has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling, pain, and redness associated with inflamed gums.

Antioxidant properties: Allspice essential oil contains antioxidants like flavonoids and terpenoids that can help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Allspice roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.
Barks: The barks of the allspice plant have antibacterial properties that combat gum infection, making it an effective remedy for inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pimenta dioica leaves at dawn on dry days for maximum potency.
Dry the leaves in a warm place or using a dehydrator set at low heat for 24 hours.
Steam distill 200g of dried leaves using 1000ml of water for 4 hours to produce 10ml of essential oil.
Filter the essential oil through cheesecloth and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.
Shake the essential oil well before use and apply topically to affected gums 2-3 times daily for optimal results.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat inflamed gums with herbal essential oils is to dilute a few drops of tea tree oil, clove oil, and ginger oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Gently massage the blend onto your gums using a soft-bristled toothbrush twice daily. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of these oils can help reduce swelling and promote healing. Regular use can also prevent further inflammation and promote healthy gum tissue growth.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to inflamed gums that are treated with herbal essential oils are conditions such as oral thrush, mouth sores, and dental plaque. Herbal essential oils like tea tree oil, peppermint oil, and eucalyptus oil have antifungal and antibacterial properties that help combat fungal and bacterial infections in the mouth, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. These oils can be used topically or added to oral rinses for effective treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, have been shown to combat periodontal pathogens and reduce inflammation in the gums. By reducing bacterial growth and promoting healing, Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils can help alleviate symptoms of inflamed gums, such as redness, swelling, and pain. Regular use of the oil may also help prevent gum inflammation and promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Cineole has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.
Limonene: Limonene has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections that can cause inflammation in the gums.
Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation, pain, and oxidative stress associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to make essential oils for inflamed gums because they contain compounds like terpinen-4-ol, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.

Buds: The buds of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to make essential oils for inflamed gums because they contain a high concentration of terpinen-4-ol and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation.

Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are also used to make essential oils for inflamed gums because they have been traditionally used in dental care to reduce gum inflammation and fight oral bacteria.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's main compound, eucalyptol, has been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for gum inflammation. Additionally, its decongestant properties help to reduce swelling and ease breathing, promoting overall oral health and comfort. This makes Eucalyptus globulus essential oils a popular choice for oral care and gum health.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: It helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in inflamed gums due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
α-Pinene: It exhibits antimicrobial properties that help combat the underlying bacterial infections causing gum inflammation.
Limonene: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in inflamed gums, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils for inflamed gums due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compound.
Buds: The buds are used to make essential oils for inflamed gums due to their rich content of eucalyptol and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and fight infections.
Barks: The barks are used to make essential oils for inflamed gums due to their high content of eucalyptol and other compounds that help reduce inflammation, ease pain, and prevent infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature eucalyptus globulus leaves at the peak of their aromatic oil production typically in spring or early summer.
Steam distill the leaves in a copper still using a 0.5-1% water-to-herb ratio for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a glass container and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or a coffee filter to remove any impurities and debris.
Store the eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with inflamed gums because it possesses potent anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds such as alkylamides and polyacetylenes that have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain. It also has antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation. Additionally, Echinacea's ability to boost the immune system can aid in the body's natural healing process, promoting a faster recovery from gum inflammation and potentially reducing the risk of further complications.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums.
Icariin: As a flavonoid glycoside, icariin exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, contributing to the reduction of gum inflammation and the promotion of tissue repair.

Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the gums from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby alleviating inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe inflamed gums.
Roots: The roots are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and their antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the underlying causes of inflamed gums.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and infection in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning when they are dry and free of moisture.
Steam distill the harvested flowers at 100-110°c for 3-4 hours to release their essential oils.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil using a paper filter to remove any impurities or contaminants present.
Store the final essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have been shown to reduce swelling and pain in the gums. Ginger's ability to inhibit the production of inflammatory enzymes also helps to alleviate gum inflammation. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial overgrowth in the mouth, further contributing to its anti-inflammatory effects and promoting a healthier gum environment. Regular use of Zingiber officinale essential oils may help alleviate gum inflammation and promote oral health.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols also possess anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing inflamed gums and promoting healing.
Zingerone: This constituent has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to combat inflammation and oxidative stress in inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make essential oils, as they contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections that may be causing gum inflammation.

Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale are sometimes used to make essential oils, which are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 pounds of fresh rhizomes from mature plants on a dry sunny morning.
Steam distill the harvested rhizomes at 100°c for 2 hours to obtain distillate.
Mix the distillate with 10 pounds of hexane in a 1:1 ratio in a 30-minute mixing process.
Filter the hexane mixture through a 50-micron filter paper to separate the essential oil.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at 15°c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's main component, caryophyllene, has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums. Additionally, the oil's antiseptic properties help combat bacteria that can cause gum infections, while its analgesic properties alleviate pain and discomfort. By promoting a healthy balance of oral flora, Syzygium aromaticum essential oils can help soothe and calm inflamed gums, promoting a faster recovery and improved oral health.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: Helps with inflamed gums by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, reducing swelling and preventing bacterial growth.
Caryophyllene: Contributes to reducing inflammation and promoting healing by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent and stimulating the release of anti-inflammatory compounds.
Limonene: Helps to alleviate inflamed gums by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, reducing swelling and preventing bacterial growth.
remedy_parts: Buds: The buds are used to make essential oils as they contain high concentrations of caryophyllene and eugenol, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help soothe inflamed gums.

Flowers: The flowers are used to make essential oils due to their content of linalool and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in inflamed gums.

Stems: The stems are used to make essential oils as they contain high amounts of alpha-pinene and beta-pinene, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10kg of fresh syzygium aromaticum flowers and leaves from mature plants to ensure optimal oil content.
Steam distill the harvested syzygium aromaticum material at 100-110 degrees celsius for 4-6 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from water using a centrifuge or decanter.
Filter the essential oil through activated charcoal to remove impurities and improve its color and clarity.
Store the extracted syzygium aromaticum essential oil in a cool dark place in glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like thujone and camphor, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth in the mouth. By reducing the bacterial load and soothing the inflamed tissues, Salvia officinalis essential oils can help alleviate symptoms of gum inflammation, such as redness and swelling. remedy_constituents: Carnosic Acid: This polyphenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.
Rosmarinic Acid: This phenolic compound exhibits strong antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, making it effective in combating gum infections and reducing inflammation.
Ursolic Acid: This triterpenoid compound possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation, prevent infection, and promote healing in inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves from Salvia officinalis are the most commonly used part to make essential oils for inflamed gums because of their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.

Stems: Stems of Salvia officinalis can be used to produce essential oils, which possess antimicrobial properties that aid in soothing inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salvia officinalis leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content and flavor.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to release essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a separating funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 5-micron filter to remove impurities and debris.
Store the filtered salvia officinalis essential oil in dark glass bottles at a temperature below 20°c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Commiphora molmol essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil, extracted from the resin of the Commiphora tree, contains a high concentration of alpha-pinene, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that cause gum infections. By reducing swelling and promoting a healthy balance of oral bacteria, Commiphora molmol essential oils can help alleviate symptoms of inflamed gums, promoting a clean and healthy mouth. Regular use may also prevent future gum infections.
remedy_constituents: β-Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.
α-Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating gum inflammation and preventing infection.
Oleic acid: This fatty acid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Buds: Rich in volatile oils, particularly myrrh resin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Barks: Containing myrrh resin, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe inflamed gums.
Leaves: Also containing myrrh resin, which helps to reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 kg of fresh commiphora molmol resin by cutting branches during full moon.
Steam distill the harvested resin at 40-60 mmhg pressure to obtain initial distillate.
Separate the initial distillate into essential oil and hydrosol by decantation.
Centrifuge the essential oil to remove any remaining hydrosol and impurities for 30 minutes.
Filter the centrifuged essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter to obtain final product.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to reduce swelling and combat bacterial growth associated with gum inflammation. By fighting off bacterial infections and soothing irritated tissues, Cinnamomum zeylanicum essential oils can promote a healthy oral environment, alleviating symptoms of inflamed gums and potentially preventing further complications. This natural remedy supports gum health and overall oral well-being.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.
Cinnamyl acetate: It possesses antimicrobial properties that prevent the growth of bacteria responsible for gum inflammation and infection.
Eugenol: It acts as an analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, providing relief from pain and inflammation in the gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain a high concentration of eugenol, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Cinnamomum zeylanicum contain eugenol and other compounds that help reduce gum inflammation and pain, making it an effective component in essential oil blends.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 kg of fresh cinnamomum zeylanicum bark and clean it thoroughly with water.
Dry the bark in a warm place for 2-3 days to reduce the moisture content to 15%.
Steam distill the dried bark in a distillation apparatus for 8 hours to extract the oil.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a separator funnel.
Filter the oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and obtain the final product.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, including limonene and geraniol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that can cause gum inflammation. The oil's refreshing and calming properties also help to soothe irritated gums, promoting a healthy environment for gum healing. Regular use of Cymbopogon citratus essential oil may help to alleviate gum inflammation and promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Helps reduce inflammation and antimicrobial properties, which aid in treating inflamed gums by killing bacteria and reducing redness.
Geraniol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate inflammation and prevent further damage to the gums.
Limonene: Shows antimicrobial properties and ability to reduce inflammation, making it effective in combating the bacteria that cause gum inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of essential oils due to their high concentration of citral, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Cymbopogon citratus contain a high amount of essential oils, which can help reduce inflammation and soothe gum pain.
Buds: Young buds of the plant are often used to create essential oils, as they have a higher concentration of active compounds that can help alleviate gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves on a sunny morning for optimal oil content and flavor.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at a temperature range of 100-150°c for 2 hours with 0.5l of water.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel.
Store the collected essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for 24 hours to settle.
Filter the stored essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any impurities before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil contains curcumin, a bioactive compound that inhibits the production of enzymes responsible for inflammation, thereby reducing swelling and pain in the gums. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause gum inflammation. Its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial actions work synergistically to promote healthy gum tissue and reduce inflammation, providing relief from gum inflammation and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with inflamed gums by reducing inflammation and suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thus alleviating discomfort and pain.
Diferuloylmethane: It helps with inflamed gums by exhibiting potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect gum tissue from damage and promote healing.
Bisabolol: It helps with inflamed gums by demonstrating anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which help to reduce swelling, kill bacteria, and prevent infection.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part for essential oil extraction due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are also used to make essential oils, which are rich in sesquiterpenes that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
Stems: Stems of Curcuma longa contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory properties, including sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds that help to soothe inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Steam distill 500 grams of dried turmeric roots with 5 liters of distilled water for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled steam and allow it to condense into a liquid mixture for 1 hour.
Separate the essential oil from the liquid mixture through decantation or filtration for 30 minutes.
Transfer the separated essential oil into a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for storage.
Store the essential oil in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight and heat sources indefinitely.
